policy.defined_by: nSealr specs contracts and vectors, not shared source code.
policy.implementation_rule: A feature may be absent, forbidden, or hardware-blocked on a solution. When the same feature is active on more than one solution, each implementation must use the same contract_id and pass the same conformance vectors unless an approved equivalent is documented in specs.

features.behavior: The implementation rejects malformed, unsafe, oversized, or unsupported signing requests before review, approval, signing, or transport output.

Every event kind uses the same structured review of kind, created_at, signer author, complete content, complete tags, request id, and approval_digest. Kind-specific summaries may add context but may not replace the universal structure.
Long content and tags are exposed through deterministic detail pages or an equivalent scroll model without truncation, hidden fields, or ambiguous wrapped items.
The local approval action is bound to the exact canonical review material through a deterministic approval_digest.
A real signing decision requires an intentional local approval gesture distinct from navigation, paging, connection, or request receipt.
Nostr sign_event produces a NIP-01 event id and BIP-340 Schnorr signature over secp256k1 using the approved event template and signer public key.
Static QR requests decode to the same validated signing request object as every other transport.
Animated QR requests are reassembled with explicit bounds and integrity checks before the decoded request reaches validation.
Airgapped responses encode signed events or deterministic errors for host-side verification without exposing secret material.
Secret material exists only in RAM for the current signing session and is not persisted by the device.

QR vaults import SeedSigner Standard SeedQR or CompactSeedQR BIP-39 seed material into the RAM-only session keyring for NIP-06 Nostr derivation after a secret-hidden source review, without Bitcoin descriptors, xpubs, PSBTs, wallet policy, or persistent storage.

QR vaults import canonical NIP-19 nsec private keys into the RAM-only session keyring for direct migration or recovery after a secret-hidden source review, without persistent key slots, policy records, mnemonics, or NIP-49 backup semantics.

Stateless QR vaults can generate BIP-39 mnemonic or standalone NIP-19 nsec session sources locally in RAM, require an explicit local backup/export ceremony before use, and never persist generated secret material.

The implementation never signs automatically; every sign_event decision requires route-specific explicit approval through local physical approval on trusted-display devices or external review acknowledgement for display-less custody.
The trusted device displays the material that controls the signing decision instead of relying on host-rendered approval text.
USB or serial frames use explicit size bounds, checksums, and deterministic parse errors before request validation.
Already-decrypted NIP-46 requests are converted into standard nSealr signing requests or deterministic local decisions without implementing relay sessions or encryption.
Automation is limited to explicit account, route, method, kind, client, time, and revocation policy; denied or unknown cases return deterministic non-signing decisions.
The solution stores or unlocks long-lived secret material only inside the defined custody boundary and never exports it through normal signing flows.
Smartcard communication uses versioned APDUs with deterministic status words and no trusted-review claim from the card alone.
Display-less custody devices require an explicit external review acknowledgement before signing is requested from the custody device.
Firmware hardening defines boot, flash, debug, provisioning, and recovery gates before production signing claims.
The host verifies signed event id, public key, signature, and approved template equivalence before accepting device output.

raspberry_qr_vault.label: Raspberry/Pi Stateless QR Vault
raspberry_qr_vault.product_goal: A SeedSigner-like airgapped Nostr signer for Raspberry/Pi hardware with RAM-only key custody and QR request/response workflows.

request_validation_v0.notes: The Python flow consumes shared signing-request and invalid-vector contracts.

nostr_event_review_universal.notes: Review is hardware-agnostic today and must remain behaviorally aligned with the ESP32 QR vault.

review_detail_pages.notes: Detail-page vectors cover long content and tags without truncation.

approval_digest_binding.notes: Approval transcripts bind the reviewed material to the approval digest.

physical_approval.notes: The approval transcript exists; real GPIO button adapters remain pending.

sign_event_bip340.notes: Desktop tests sign fixture events; Raspberry hardware acceptance remains pending.

qr_static_request.notes: Static QR envelope behavior is covered by shared transport vectors.

qr_animated_request.notes: Animated QR flow uses the shared chunking and integrity contract.

qr_response.notes: Signed responses and errors are encoded for host verification.

stateless_session_custody.notes: Package-owned session keyring slots are RAM-only, store mutable internal copies of imported sources, and wipe those internal slots on clear/destruction as best-effort Python process hygiene. Real Pi power-cycle/session-loss evidence and hardware lifecycle acceptance remain pending.

seedqr_session_import.notes: Standard SeedQR and CompactSeedQR parser/signing harnesses are implemented through shared vectors for RAM-only NIP-06 session loading, and package tests consume the shared secret-hidden session import review pages and import approval digest. Final Pi camera/display/button import UX remains pending.

nip19_nsec_session_import.notes: NIP-19 nsec parser/signing harnesses are implemented through shared vectors for direct RAM-only session loading, and package tests consume the shared secret-hidden session import review pages and import approval digest. Final Pi camera/display/button import UX remains pending.

session_source_generation.notes: Package-owned helpers can generate 12- or 24-word BIP-39 sources and standalone nsec-equivalent private-key sources into the RAM-only session-source boundary with injected entropy for tests. Final Pi backup/export UI, physical review, and power-cycle evidence remain pending.

manual_only_policy.notes: The vault line has no automated signing grants.

device_display_review.notes: The review model exists; real display adapters remain pending.

serial_usb_transport.notes: The Raspberry QR vault is an airgapped QR signer, not a USB signer.

nip46_decrypted_bridge.notes: NIP-46 is handled by companion infrastructure, not by the airgapped QR vault.

scoped_policy_automation.notes: Automatic signing would violate the manual-only stateless vault model.

persistent_secret_custody.notes: The vault must not persist long-lived secret material.

smartcard_apdu.notes: The Raspberry QR vault does not use smartcard APDUs.

external_review_acknowledgement.notes: The device itself provides local review and approval.

secure_boot_hardening.notes: Host OS and image hardening can improve deployment, but it is not protection for persisted vault secrets.

response_verification.notes: The companion verifies QR vault outputs against shared signed-event fixtures.

esp32_qr_vault.label: ESP32 Stateless QR Vault
esp32_qr_vault.product_goal: An airgapped ESP32-S3 QR signer with camera, display, local controls, RAM-only key custody, and behavior matching the Raspberry QR vault where features overlap.

request_validation_v0.notes: Host-core tests enforce bounded parsing and unsafe event-template rejection before signing can be enabled.

nostr_event_review_universal.notes: Display-frame behavior uses the same universal event review contract as Raspberry.

review_detail_pages.notes: The display UI supports top-level pages and detail scrolling for complete content and tags.

approval_digest_binding.notes: The ESP32 host-core binds review frames to approval_digest before disabled signing response.

physical_approval.notes: The host-core approval state exists; production button/touch drivers remain pending.

sign_event_bip340.notes: Real signing remains blocked until camera, display, buttons, provisioning, and hardening gates pass.

qr_static_request.notes: Decoded static QR behavior is modeled; real camera ingestion on the final QR board remains pending.

qr_animated_request.notes: Chunk reassembly behavior is modeled; camera-frame acceptance remains pending.

qr_response.notes: Host-core can encode already-produced response JSON into static nsealr1 and animated nsealr1a QR envelopes against the shared response vector. Real signing, response QR display hardware, camera scan-back, and hardware acceptance remain pending.

stateless_session_custody.notes: Host-core now has a bounded RAM-only session keyring model for already parsed nsec and BIP-39 key sources, wipes active source slots on clear/destruction, disables keyring copy/move operations, and consumes shared secret-hidden import review summaries with deterministic review_id, fingerprints, and import approval digests. Camera/import UX, hardware reset behavior, hardware review acceptance, NIP-06 derivation wiring, response QR output, and real signing remain pending.

seedqr_session_import.notes: Host-core decodes SeedSigner Standard SeedQR and CompactSeedQR into checked BIP-39 word indexes against the shared vector, can render those checked indexes back to mnemonic words, and consumes the shared secret-hidden session import review vectors for pages, review_id, fingerprint, and import approval digest. Camera QR input, NIP-06 derivation, hardware import-review acceptance, response QR output, and signing remain pending.

nip19_nsec_session_import.notes: Host-core decodes canonical NIP-19 nsec payloads against the shared vector, the bounded session keyring can hold parsed nsec material, and consumes the shared secret-hidden session import review vectors without exposing raw private-key bytes. Camera QR input, hardware import-review acceptance, response QR output, and signing remain pending.

session_source_generation.notes: Host-core can generate BIP-39 sources from 16- or 32-byte entropy and standalone nsec-equivalent private-key sources from valid secp256k1 scalar entropy into the same RAM-only source/review boundary. Hardware RNG wiring, backup/export UI, hardware review acceptance, reset-loss evidence, response QR output, and real signing remain pending.

manual_only_policy.notes: The QR vault target must not use automated signing grants.

device_display_review.notes: Display rendering is validated on T-Display S3; final QR-board display acceptance remains pending.

serial_usb_transport.notes: The ESP32 QR vault product uses QR transport, not connected USB signing.

nip46_decrypted_bridge.notes: NIP-46 bridge behavior is handled by the companion and USB signer line.

scoped_policy_automation.notes: Automatic signing would violate the manual-only stateless vault model.

persistent_secret_custody.notes: The QR vault must not persist long-lived secret material.

smartcard_apdu.notes: The ESP32 QR vault does not use smartcard APDUs.

external_review_acknowledgement.notes: The ESP32 QR vault provides local review and approval on the device.

secure_boot_hardening.notes: Secure boot, flash encryption, and debug lock are hardening gates, not protection for persistent QR-vault secrets.

response_verification.notes: Host verification for ESP32 QR outputs must match the Raspberry QR vault contract.

esp32_usb_nip46.label: ESP32 USB/NIP-46 Signer
esp32_usb_nip46.product_goal: A daily-use connected ESP32-S3 signer with USB/display review, optional scoped policy automation through the companion, and real signing disabled until hardening gates pass.

request_validation_v0.notes: USB and NIP-46 converted requests must pass the same standard request validator.

nostr_event_review_universal.notes: Connected signing still uses the universal event review model before local approval.

review_detail_pages.notes: The same detail-page model is used where the display has enough navigation capacity.

approval_digest_binding.notes: The disabled signing flow already binds approval_digest in host-core tests.

physical_approval.notes: Physical approval semantics exist; production hardware controls remain pending.

sign_event_bip340.notes: Real ESP32 sign_event remains intentionally disabled until acceptance gates pass.

qr_static_request.notes: The USB/NIP-46 signer is a connected transport line, not a QR vault.

qr_animated_request.notes: Animated QR belongs to stateless QR vault lines.

qr_response.notes: Connected responses return over USB or companion channels.

stateless_session_custody.notes: The USB signer is a persistent-secret device family, not a stateless QR vault.

seedqr_session_import.notes: SeedQR RAM-only session import belongs to stateless QR vault lines, not the persistent USB signer.

nip19_nsec_session_import.notes: NIP-19 nsec RAM-only session import belongs to stateless QR vault lines, not the persistent USB signer.

session_source_generation.notes: RAM-only QR-vault source generation is not the persistent USB/NIP-46 signer account-generation model; persistent seed/key creation belongs under persistent-secret custody and policy-gated provisioning.

manual_only_policy.notes: Manual review remains the default fallback when no explicit scoped policy permits automation.

device_display_review.notes: Display review is implemented on the current development board and must pass production acceptance.

serial_usb_transport.notes: The transport parser uses bounded frames and deterministic errors.

nip46_decrypted_bridge.notes: The companion supports already-decrypted payload conversion; full relay sessions are out of scope for the current gate.

scoped_policy_automation.notes: Scoped automation is planned only after the pre-signing hardening contract is complete.

persistent_secret_custody.notes: Provisioning and storage design remain acceptance gates before production signing.

smartcard_apdu.notes: The ESP32 USB signer does not expose the JavaCard APDU protocol.

external_review_acknowledgement.notes: The signer has its own display and physical approval controls.

secure_boot_hardening.notes: A validated development-only security profile and read-only eFuse audit path exist, and signing status reports secure_boot, flash_encryption, and debug_lock as blockers. Secure boot, flash encryption, debug locking, provisioning, recovery policy, persistent-secret storage, and production signing remain disabled or pending.

response_verification.notes: The companion verifies signed outputs and disabled-signing errors before accepting them.

smartcard.label: JavaCard/NFC Smartcard Signer
smartcard.product_goal: A display-less card custody line that can sign through APDUs only after external review acknowledgement and deterministic policy checks.

request_validation_v0.notes: Host-side validation must happen before APDU signing requests reach the card.

nostr_event_review_universal.notes: The card cannot provide trusted display review; an external reviewer can use the same universal contract.

review_detail_pages.notes: External review tools should use the same content and tag detail-page semantics.

approval_digest_binding.notes: The external review acknowledgement should bind the reviewed request to the APDU signing operation.

physical_approval.notes: A standard display-less smartcard has no local screen/button approval model.

sign_event_bip340.notes: Simulator signing exists; card profile and hardware behavior remain under development.

qr_static_request.notes: The smartcard line does not use QR request transport.

qr_animated_request.notes: Animated QR belongs to stateless QR vault lines.

stateless_session_custody.notes: The smartcard line is persistent custody, not a RAM-only vault.

seedqr_session_import.notes: SeedQR RAM-only session import belongs to stateless QR vault lines, not display-less smartcard custody.

nip19_nsec_session_import.notes: NIP-19 nsec RAM-only session import belongs to stateless QR vault lines, not display-less smartcard custody.

session_source_generation.notes: RAM-only QR-vault source generation is not a display-less smartcard custody feature.

manual_only_policy.notes: Display-less smartcard signing must remain manual-only with explicit external review acknowledgement.

device_display_review.notes: The card is display-less and must not claim trusted event review by itself.

serial_usb_transport.notes: The smartcard line uses card transports rather than nSealr serial USB frames.

nip46_decrypted_bridge.notes: The companion can route approved, already-decrypted NIP-46 requests to a card-backed signer.

scoped_policy_automation.notes: A display-less smartcard must not be treated as a trusted policy-automation signer without a later card-side policy contract.

persistent_secret_custody.notes: The card is the intended persistent-secret boundary.

smartcard_apdu.notes: Shared simulator and APDU vectors define deterministic card responses.

external_review_acknowledgement.notes: Display-less signing requires explicit external review acknowledgement before card signing.

secure_boot_hardening.notes: Smartcard platform assurance is tracked through card profile and APDU behavior, not ESP32 firmware hardening.

response_verification.notes: Host tools must verify card-produced signed events before returning them.

custom_hardware_wallet.label: Custom Nostr Hardware Wallet With Persistent Secret
custom_hardware_wallet.product_goal: A research family for a purpose-built persistent-secret Nostr hardware wallet, now centered on a USB-C bus-powered TROPIC01-assisted Rev A scaffold before any KiCad or production claim.

request_validation_v0.notes: The custom wallet must inherit the same request validation contract before any production claim.

nostr_event_review_universal.notes: A final hardware wallet must show the same universal review material on trusted hardware.

review_detail_pages.notes: Display and navigation design must cover complete content and tags.

approval_digest_binding.notes: The wallet must bind approval to the exact reviewed request.

physical_approval.notes: Final hardware must provide deliberate local approve/reject controls.

sign_event_bip340.notes: Rev A keeps BIP-340 signing on the ESP32-S3 host MCU; direct TROPIC01 Schnorr remains future-gated until a public API, firmware release, or written vendor path is verified.

qr_static_request.notes: A custom wallet may support QR transport in addition to connected transport.

qr_animated_request.notes: Animated QR support is optional and must use the shared transport contract if present.

qr_response.notes: QR response support is optional and must use the same verification contract if present.

stateless_session_custody.notes: This family is explicitly persistent-secret hardware, not a stateless vault.

seedqr_session_import.notes: SeedQR RAM-only session import belongs to stateless QR vault lines; persistent import belongs under persistent-secret custody design.

nip19_nsec_session_import.notes: NIP-19 nsec RAM-only session import belongs to stateless QR vault lines; persistent import belongs under persistent-secret custody design.

session_source_generation.notes: RAM-only QR-vault source generation is not the custom persistent-secret wallet generation model; persistent seed/key creation belongs under persistent-secret custody and TROPIC01-assisted provisioning research.

manual_only_policy.notes: Manual-only mode should remain available even if scoped automation is later supported.

device_display_review.notes: Trusted device review is mandatory for production hardware-wallet claims.

serial_usb_transport.notes: Connected transport may use the same bounded serial frame contract.

nip46_decrypted_bridge.notes: The companion may route already-decrypted NIP-46 requests to this family after policy gates exist.

scoped_policy_automation.notes: Automation must remain explicit, scoped, revocable, and off by default.

persistent_secret_custody.notes: Rev A researches TROPIC01 secure channel, TRNG, pairing lifecycle, MAC-and-Destroy PIN hardening, key wrapping/unlock material, and device authenticity.

smartcard_apdu.notes: The custom wallet family does not use the JavaCard APDU contract unless it embeds a distinct card module.

external_review_acknowledgement.notes: A production hardware wallet is expected to provide its own trusted review screen.

secure_boot_hardening.notes: Boot, flash, debug lock, provisioning, update, recovery, and TROPIC01 power-cycle policy are core research gates before storing production secrets.

response_verification.notes: Host-side verification must be part of every custom wallet transport.
